item.title: Semiconductors: supercycle or superbubble?
item.description: Plus, semis’ fragile supply chain

item.title: How to close AI’s accountability loophole
item.description: Governance of new technologies must be determined by elected officials rather than fastest moving companies

item.title: SpaceX and the Zuckerberg discount
item.description: A founder-entrenching shareholder structure can do a great deal of harm

item.title: Superdrug IPO would put lipstick on a drab UK retail sector
item.description: Superdrug grew almost twice as fast as Boots over past four years

item.title: Why Europe must embrace tariffs
item.description: Sorry folks, the rules aren’t coming to save you

item.title: America’s hegemonic glory is under threat
item.description: Along with excessive ‘guns and butter’ spending, that risk means long-term Treasuries are expensive

item.title: What are my rights when buying a house with my unmarried partner?
item.description: We will both pay towards the mortgage, but it won’t be a 50/50 split

item.title: No, now is not the time for UK rent controls
item.description: Three think-tanks are promoting curbs to rental growth — but that horse has already bolted

item.title: My night at London’s most expensive sushi restaurant
item.description: ‘Each course at Sushi Kanesaka is delivered for evaluation, where the desired response is ‘flawless’ rather than ‘tasty’’

item.title: ‘China-maxxing’ helps spur an inbound tourism boom
item.description: Beijing has found an economic win by drawing more international visitors to the country

item.title: Uber bid for Delivery Hero would be an odd route to superapp status
item.description: Food delivery group’s taxed operating profit this year is only about 2.4 per cent of its enterprise value

item.title: For ailing Lululemon, going private might not be a stretch
item.description: The company has essentially no net debt and still generated $1bn in free cash flow last year

item.title: Preventing a ‘Chernobyl moment’ in AI
item.description: A White House order on testing frontier models would be a significant first step

item.title: Want to predict central banker behaviour? Look to their birth date
item.description: Formative experiences shape our views on future inflation as much as the data

item.title: My five lessons from judging this year’s International Booker Prize
item.description: Reading 128 books in eight months was a privilege — and a lasting education in making time, embracing difference and building literary muscle

item.title: Securitisation could help plug Europe’s funding gap
item.description: Deeper capital markets are essential to the continent’s growth

item.title: How Brexit made Britain a European country
item.description: With its political chaos and high-spending state, the UK is more continental than it was a decade ago

item.title: Japan fails to grapple with past in Shōwa spectacle
item.description: The celebrations were enthusiastic but the country seems to have a hard time calibrating its relationship with what went before

item.title: Blair’s diagnosis of Labour is correct
item.description: Party’s commitment to ambitious pledges before election narrowed its room for manoeuvre

item.title: AI’s mixed economic signals
item.description: Plus, the euro convergence trade

item.title: Halifax and TSB have outlived their brand-name usefulness
item.description: Bank consolidation in Britain now makes sense in targeting customers for multiple products

item.title: How did Jay Powell do at the Fed?
item.description: The outgoing chair has made some mistakes, but his decision to stand up to Trump was heroic

item.title: The surprising power of simple predictions
item.description: Experts can be sidetracked by elaborate ideas, overlooking the direct approach

item.title: The pizza oven start-up that came through the fire
item.description: Co-founders of Scotland’s Ooni faced a post-pandemic crisis after invention went global

item.title: How some blockchains enable front-running
item.description: Regulators need to tackle the issue and address the cost to investors of excessive transparency
